3.   <PropertyGroup>
4.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
5.     <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
6.     <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
7.     <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
8.     <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
9.     <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
10.     <Company>Contoso</Company>
11.     <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
12.   </PropertyGroup>
13. 
14.   <ItemGroup>
15.     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.0" />

17.   </ItemGroup>
18. 
19.   <PropertyGroup>
20.     <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
21.   </PropertyGroup>
22. 
23.   <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
24.     <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
25.   </Target>
26. 
27. </Project>

data.content:
Here's my analysis of the build infrastructure across all 5 projects:

---

## Build Infrastructure Review

### 🔴 Issue 1: No `Directory.Build.props` — Massive Property Duplication

All 5 projects repeat these identical properties:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Fix:** Create a root `Directory.Build.props` with shared properties, and a `test/Directory.Build.props` for test-specific settings (`IsPackable=false`).

---

### 🔴 Issue 2: No Central Package Management — Version Drift Already Present

Package versions are scattered across projects and **have already diverged**:

| Package | Core | Api | Services | Api.Tests | Core.Tests |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Logging.Abstractions` | 8.0.0 | 8.0.0 | **8.0.1** ⚠️ | — | — |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | — | 17.9.0 | **17.10.0** ⚠️ |
| `xunit` | — | — | — | 2.7.1 | **2.8.1** ⚠️ |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | — | — | — | 2.5.6 | **2.8.1** ⚠️ |

**Fix:** Create `Directory.Packages.props` with `<ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>` and define all versions once via `<PackageVersion>` items.

---

### 🟡 Issue 3: No `Directory.Build.targets` — Duplicated Custom Target

The `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted identically in all 3 src projects:

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Fix:** Move to a root `Directory.Build.targets` so it applies to all projects automatically.

---

### 🟡 Issue 4: `DocumentationFile` Duplicated in All Src Projects

All 3 src projects define the same second `<PropertyGroup>`:

```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

**Fix:** Move into `Directory.Build.props` (conditionally for non-test projects, or in `src/Directory.Build.props`).

---

### 🟡 Issue 5: No `global.json`

Without it, any SDK version on the build machine will be used. This risks build inconsistencies across environments.

**Fix:** Add `global.json` at the repo root pinning the SDK version (e.g., `8.0.xxx`).

---

### 🟢 Issue 6: Test Projects Duplicate Common Test Packages

Both test projects independently reference `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, `xunit`, `xunit.runner.visualstudio`, and `NSubstitute`. When a new test project is added, someone must remember to copy all of these.

**Fix:** A `test/Directory.Build.props` can automatically include common test package references for any project under `test/`.

---

### Recommended File Structure

```
ContosoApp.sln
global.json                      ← pin SDK version
Directory.Build.props            ← shared properties for all projects
Directory.Build.targets          ← shared targets (PrintBuildInfo)
Directory.Packages.props         ← central package version management
src/
  Directory.Build.props          ← src-specific (DocumentationFile)
  ContosoApp.Core/
  ContosoApp.Api/
  ContosoApp.Services/
test/
  Directory.Build.props          ← test-specific (IsPackable, test packages)
  ContosoApp.Api.Tests/
  ContosoApp.Core.Tests/
```

After this refactoring, individual `.csproj` files would shrink to just their SDK type, project references, and project-specific packages — typically 5–10 lines instead of 25+.

Would you like me to implement these changes?
